name: Grapefruit Whipped Tahini and Chickpeas (Humus)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon garlic peeled and crushed with salt
1/2 teaspoon lemon zest, or to taste
1/4 to 1/2 cup ice water (up to 1/2 cup)
16 ounces cooked chickpeas, rinsed and drained
1 tablespoon pine nuts, dry pan roasted
recipeInstructions:
Makes about 2-1/2 cups. Whisk the juices, water, garlic, zest, and tahini in a tall container until smooth. Add ice water gradually until the mixture is white and creamy. Stir in the chickpeas (garbanzos) and blend to the desired consistency. Season with additional salt if needed, and stir in the coriander cohort blend. Top with lightly toasted pine nuts, more lemon juice, and/or a 1/2 tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil. Store tightly covered in the refrigerator up to 5 days.
As listed for appetizer: 166 calories, 22% fat (4.3 g).
Appetizer: Serve hummus with warm pita bread and radish slices.
Mediterranean style salad pockets:
Kashi salad with yogurt dressing
Radishes
Thinly sliced napa cabbage
Use the hummus as a spread for the salad in the pocket.
Dressing: Thin 1 tablespoon of juice (such as carrot, apple, or citrus) over a rice-pilaf salad, potato and cucumber salad with steamed asparagus, etc.
